## [00:00 - 03:01]

This is a deep dive into custom events. Custom events are one of my favorite things. And so if you're not familiar with what custom events are, briefly, think of standard events, first of all. So standard events are the predefined conversions that you can track, and send to Facebook or Meta for the purposes of optimization, reporting, targeting. Okay. Standard events include things like purchase, lead, complete registration, search, etc. Custom events will be those things that don't fall into those predefined buckets. Okay. So you define what this is. It's something that's super important to you. And because of that, you want to track it and use it for your advertising and targeting and optimization, reporting, etc. So I find custom events to be really useful because, for example, I want to know when there's been a quality website visitor. Maybe they didn't buy anything. Maybe they didn't hit a product page, become a lead, etc. But there are other things that they can do that are important. Okay. And especially as a blogger and someone who creates regular content, I care about people who visit and scroll, spend more time, interact with specific things.
